answer:
**Kalpabuddha** (ch. 6) culminates its esoteric exposition by claiming that the five Tathāgatas (Pañcatathāgata)—Akṣobhya, Ratnasambhava, Amitābha, Amoghasiddhi, and Vairocana—return from their realms (*lokadhātu*) to indwell the practitioner's body as the five wisdoms (*pañcajñāna*: *cakṣus*, *śrotra*, *ghrāṇa*, *jihvā*, *hṛdaya*), which manifest as the five senses (*pañcendriya*: eyes, ears, nose, mouth, liver/heart/mind), together with their *devī*s, forming the "Divine Five-Souls" (*Pañcātma*) that constitute the self.[^1]<sup title="read_chapter citation for ch. 6; key claim in [p-c8567e]">[works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/06/translation/](#p-c8567e)</sup> This "supreme secret" integrates macrocosmic deities into microcosmic physiology as innate gnosis (*jñāna*), but it does not reference *kevala-jñāna* (or *kaivalya*) explicitly—searches for "kevala" yielded zero results across the corpus.[^2]

**San Hyan Mahājñāna** (ch. 1, its sole chapter) teaches *tattva*-jñāna across states of consciousness (*jāgrat*/waking, *svapna*/dream, *suṣupti*/deep sleep, *tūrya*/fourth), escalating to *kaivalya*, *tūryānta*, and *paraṃ kaivalya* (supreme isolation), equated with *paramātmā*, *nirāśraya* (supportless), and union with Paramaśiva beyond *prakṛti*/*puruṣa*, *vyakta*/*avyakta*, and *saṃsāra*—the "void within the void," *mokṣa*, and "Sacred Supreme Wisdom" (*saṅ hyaṅ mahājñāna*).[^3]<sup title="read_chapter citation for ch. 1; central claims in [p-9194a6] (kaivalya), [p-7777f3] (state-gods), [p-0f7a95] (end)">[works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-9194a6]</sup> Its key passage maps gods (Brahma, Viṣṇu, Rudra, Maheśvara, Mahādeva, Īśvara, Paramaśiva) onto these states culminating in liberation.[^4]

These do not name the *same end-state*: *Kalpabuddha* (Vajrayāna-inflected, five-Buddha *pañcajñāna* as embodied self) emphasizes tantric immanence of wisdom-deities in senses, while *San Hyan Mahājñāna* (Śaiva) progresses through Vedāntic/Yogic states to transcendent *paramakaivalya* in Śiva. The former's gnosis (*pañcajñāna*) aligns more with a *milestone* (integrated sensory wisdom) en route to the latter's *terminus* (isolation from manifestation), as corpus stats link them (cosine 0.15)[^5] and both invoke *jñāna*, but no text equates them—*Mahājñāna* treats *kaivalya* as a higher progression beyond lower *tattva*s.[^6]

[^1]: [works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/06/translation/#p-c8567e] (core passage); full ch. [works/unknown-kalpabuddha-d760dc/06/translation/].

[^2]: Corpus-wide search: `{"query": "\"kevala\" OR \"kewala\" OR \"kaivalya-jnana\""}` → 0 results; *Kalpabuddha* hits only generic *jñāna*.

[^3]: [works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-9194a6]; cf. [p-7777f3], [p-cf67ae], [p-0f7a95] (colophon).

[^4]: Full ch. [works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/].

[^5]: *Kalpabuddha* wiki: nearest works include *San Hyan Mahajnana* (cosine 0.15); vice versa (0.23 via *Kamahayanikan* proxy).

[^6]: *Mahājñāna* wiki ch.1 key: states → *kaivalya* → *paraṃ kaivalya* as "seven skies"; no *pañcatathāgata*/*pañcajñāna*.
